name: Human Physiology
description: Discover how the systems of the human body work together to maintain balance and keep you alive.

questions.question: Which organ system is primarily responsible for transporting oxygen, nutrients, and waste products throughout the human body?
questions.explain: The circulatory system, composed of the heart and blood vessels, pumps and moves blood across the whole body.

questions.question: What is the primary function of the red blood cells in the human body?
options: To carry oxygen to body tissues
To help form blood clots after an injury
questions.explain: Red blood cells contain hemoglobin, a protein specialized in binding and carrying oxygen from the lungs to the rest of the body.

questions.question: In which part of the human digestive system does most of the chemical digestion and nutrient absorption take place?
questions.explain: While digestion begins in the mouth and stomach, the small intestine is where most enzymes break down food and nutrients enter the bloodstream.

questions.question: What process describes the body's ability to maintain a stable internal environment despite external changes?
questions.explain: Homeostasis regulates internal conditions like temperature and fluid balance to keep body systems functioning correctly.

questions.question: Which gaseous waste product is removed from the blood by the lungs during exhalation?
questions.explain: Cells produce carbon dioxide as waste during cellular respiration, which blood carries back to the lungs to be exhaled.

questions.question: What is the primary functional unit of the human kidney responsible for filtering blood and forming urine?
questions.explain: Each kidney contains millions of microscopic structures called nephrons that filter waste materials out of the bloodstream.

questions.question: Which part of the central nervous system controls involuntary functions such as heartbeat, breathing, and blood pressure?
questions.explain: The brainstem connects the brain to the spinal cord and manages autonomic, life-sustaining functions.

questions.question: What chemical messengers are produced by the endocrine glands and released directly into the bloodstream?
questions.explain: Hormones travel through the circulatory system to send signals to distant organs and regulate processes like growth and metabolism.

questions.question: Where does the gas exchange take place inside the human respiratory system?
questions.explain: Alveoli are tiny, thin-walled air sacs at the end of the bronchioles where oxygen entering the lungs enters the blood and carbon dioxide leaves it.

questions.question: Which type of muscle tissue is found exclusively in the walls of the human heart?
questions.explain: Cardiac muscle is an involuntary, striated tissue specialized solely for pumping blood throughout the lifetime of the organism.
